subject: Chemistry
title: Alchemical Symbols and Secret Codes
description: A micro-lesson that makes Alchemical Symbols and Secret Codes usable.

hook.text: Alchemists wrote in code—using symbols for elements, planets for metals, and dragons for chemical reactions.

buildup.text: Gold was the Sun (☉), silver was the Moon (☽), copper was Venus (♀), and iron was Mars (♂).

discovery.text: They encoded recipes in allegorical images: a green lion eating the sun meant acid dissolving gold.

twist.text: The secrecy was deliberate. Alchemists feared persecution—and wanted to keep profitable secrets from competitors.

climax.text: Modern chemists still use some alchemical symbols. ♀ and ♂ for copper and iron became the symbols for female and male.

punchline.text: Planets for metals, dragons for reactions. Chemistry's first language was art.

quiz.question: In alchemical symbolism, what did a green lion eating the sun represent?
